zh.lifeSummary: 當William Russell於1557年出生在英國素可洛瑟費爾德時，他的父親Thomas Russell31歲，母親Eleanor Skarth30歲。他於1586年4月18日與Mary Hilles在英國素可洛瑟費爾德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1619年9月28日在英國英格蘭易亞斯特 素可洛瑟費爾德享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: Russell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: of Norman origin, from Old French and Anglo-Norman French r(o)ussel, a diminutive of Old French rous(e) ‘red, reddish’, used either as a nickname for someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion, or as a personal name. Compare Rouse . This Norman name has been established in Ireland since the 12th century. It has been reinforced in Britain and Ireland by Huguenot bearers of the name Roussel, of the same Old French origin.

English: habitational name from any of several places called Rushall (Norfolk, Staffordshire, Wiltshire) or possibly sometimes from Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent). Rushall in Staffordshire derives from Old English rysc ‘rush, rushes’ + halh ‘nook, corner of land’. Rushall in Norfolk derives from an uncertain first element + Old English halh. Rushall in Wiltshire derives from an Old English personal name Rust (genitive Rustes) + halh. Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent) probably derives from Old English rust ‘rust, rust color’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’, but with a change in the final element due to influence from Middle English, Old English hall ‘hall, residence’, perhaps referring to a nearby building.
Americanized form of German Rüssel, from a pet form of any of various personal names formed with the element hrōd ‘fame, renown’.

variants.name: Russler
meanings: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of rüsseln ‘to shallow plow’.

variants.name: Rouse
meanings: English: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French rous(e) ‘red(-haired)’ (from Latin russ(e)us).
Walloon and northern French (Rousé): variant of Rosé (see Rose ).

variants.name: Russel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Russell  .
German: from a pet form of Russ, see Russ 2.
northwest German (Rüssel): probably a variant of Rossel .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When William Russell was born in 1557, in Rotherfield, Sussex, England, his father, Thomas Russell, was 31 and his mother, Eleanor Skarth, was 30. He married Mary Hilles on 18 April 1586, in Rotherfield, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 28 September 1619, in Rotherfield, East Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62.

Il a épousé Mary Hilles le 18 avril 1586, à Rotherfield, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 28 septembre 1619, à Rotherfield, East Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Russell(이)가 1557년 영국 수세스 로더필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Russell(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Skarth(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1586년 4월 18일 영국 수세스 로더필드에서 Mary Hilles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1619년 9월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 이스트 수세스 로더필드에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Hilles 18 апреля 1586 г. в местности ротирфиелд, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 28 сентября 1619 г. в местности ротирфиелд, эаст суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет.
